title: Change Control Completeness for Production Payment Releases

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.5.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.5.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.37: Change Control Completeness for Production Payment Releases.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.37' running on index=ci sourcetype="jenkins:build" or sourcetype="azuredevops:release"; index=itsm snow:change_request, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) if available
spl:
index=ci sourcetype="jenkins:build" earliest=-7d
| where match(job,"(?i)payment|checkout|token")
| where result="SUCCESS" AND like(build_url,"%prod%")
| eval chg_hint=replace(build_parameters,".*?(CHG\\d{7}).*","\\1")
| join type=left max=1 chg_hint [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | rename number as chg_hint
    | fields chg_hint, state
  ]
| where isnull(state) OR NOT match(state,"(?i)closed|implemented")
| table _time, job, build_url, chg_hint, state
description: Joins deployment pipeline events to approved change tickets so every production change affecting payment software follows formal change control as PCI expects for secure systems.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ci, itsm and sourcetypes azuredevops:release, jenkins:build, snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require pipeline to inject `CHG` token parameter. (2) Add Git branch allow-list. (3) Alert on null `state`. (4) Retain build logs 13 months. Indexes required: `index=ci`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=azuredevops:release`, `sourcetype=jenkins:build`, `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
visualization: Table (unlinked deploys), column chart (violations by week), single value (count). Key fields: `build_url`, `chg_hint`, `job`, `state`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) if available.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ci` `sourcetype="jenkins:build"` or `sourcetype="azuredevops:release"`; `index=itsm` `snow:change_request`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require pipeline to inject `CHG` token parameter. (2) Add Git branch allow-list. (3) Alert on null `state`. (4) Retain build logs 13 months.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ci sourcetype="jenkins:build" earliest=-7d
| where match(job,"(?i)payment|checkout|token")
| where result="SUCCESS" AND like(build_url,"%prod%")
| eval chg_hint=replace(build_parameters,".*?(CHG\\d{7}).*","\\1")
| join type=left max=1 chg_hint [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | rename number as chg_hint
    | fields chg_hint, state
  ]
| where isnull(state) OR NOT match(state,"(?i)closed|implemented")
| table _time, job, build_url, chg_hint, state
```

Understanding this SPL

**Change Control Completeness for Production Payment Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.5.1, 6.5.2)** — Joins deployment pipeline events to approved change tickets so every production change affecting payment software follows formal change control as PCI expects for secure systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ci` `sourcetype="jenkins:build"` or `sourcetype="azuredevops:release"`; `index=itsm` `snow:change_request`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) if available. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ci; **sourcetype**: jenkins:build. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ci, sourcetype="jenkins:build", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(job,"(?i)payment|checkout|token")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where result="SUCCESS" AND like(build_url,"%prod%")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **chg_hint** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(state) OR NOT match(state,"(?i)closed|implemented")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Output fields: _time, build_url, chg_hint, job, state.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unlinked deploys), column chart (violations by week), single value (count).
grandmaExplanation: We joins deployment pipeline events to approved change tickets so every production change affecting payment software follows formal change control as PCI expects for secure systems. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
